decision_sequence.label: Reassess whether an insomnia Rx is still needed after primary therapy

supplements.name: Tadalafil 2.5mg
supplements.notes: Tadalafil probably matters more through sexual-function / wellbeing utility than through proven life-extension at your baseline risk.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Tadalafil probably matters more through sexual-function / wellbeing utility than through proven life-extension at your baseline risk.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Kept meaningful on QOL because current use reveals real private value, but the mortality side is heavily shrunk because PDE5 survival data are mostly observational in older, higher-risk men.

supplements.name: APAP nightly
supplements.notes: With confirmed OSA, PAP is the most evidence-backed next sleep intervention by a wide margin.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: With confirmed OSA, PAP is the most evidence-backed next sleep intervention by a wide margin.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Your March 25, 2026 home study showed mild OSA (REI 7.7/hr), and the updated airway probability is 0.695, so PAP now gets credit from an actual diagnosis rather than only wearable inference.

supplements.name: Melatonin 300mcg
supplements.notes: Small, probably real sleep-onset benefit; unlikely to be a big standalone QALY driver.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Small, probably real sleep-onset benefit; unlikely to be a big standalone QALY driver.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Upweighted because short sleep is a live issue, but kept modest because human meta-analytic effects are measured in minutes, not hours.

supplements.name: Magnesium 200mg
supplements.notes: At your baseline, magnesium looks more like a sleep-support and small BP intervention than a major longevity lever.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: At your baseline, magnesium looks more like a sleep-support and small BP intervention than a major longevity lever.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Upweighted because your 90-day combined sleep is only 6.49 h/night; small BP benefit remains because magnesium RCTs are stronger than most supplements.

supplements.name: Head elevation nightly
supplements.notes: Head elevation is a low-risk positional airway aid with the best case in upper-airway-predominant sleep-disordered breathing.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Head elevation is a low-risk positional airway aid with the best case in upper-airway-predominant sleep-disordered breathing.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Upweighted because your recent improvement pattern is compatible with an upper-airway contributor, but kept modest because your home data do not cleanly isolate elevation from the other airway changes.

supplements.name: HIIT 2x/week
supplements.notes: Two HIIT sessions per week is still plausible as a small positive, but only if it replaces easier cardio rather than stacking on top of everything.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Two HIIT sessions per week is still plausible as a small positive, but only if it replaces easier cardio rather than stacking on top of everything.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Two weekly interval sessions can add a bit more CRF upside, but the marginal return is still capped because you already train daily and your Whoop fitness proxies are strong.

supplements.name: Tempo run 1x/week
supplements.notes: A weekly tempo run is a credible middle-ground training intervention with modest expected upside.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: A weekly tempo run is a credible middle-ground training intervention with modest expected upside.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: I place tempo work between zone 2 and HIIT: probably more additive than easy running, but not as distinct a VO2max stimulus as true intervals.

supplements.name: Nasacort nightly
supplements.notes: Nasacort looks like a phenotype-specific sleep intervention here: worthwhile if nasal inflammation or congestion is meaningfully contributing, not a generic prevention supplement.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Nasacort looks like a phenotype-specific sleep intervention here: worthwhile if nasal inflammation or congestion is meaningfully contributing, not a generic prevention supplement.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Upweighted because your recent airway-directed trial improved breathing, latency, snoring, and sleep quality, producing an airway-response signal of 0.274.

supplements.name: Glycine 2g bedtime
supplements.notes: Glycine looks like a plausible sleep/QOL helper rather than a major mortality intervention.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Glycine looks like a plausible sleep/QOL helper rather than a major mortality intervention.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Upweighted because sleep remains an active problem, but the evidence is still mainly symptom-level and measured in modest changes.

supplements.name: HIIT 1x/week
supplements.notes: One weekly HIIT session looks like a plausible way to improve VO2max/cardiorespiratory fitness a bit without assuming sedentary-person returns.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: One weekly HIIT session looks like a plausible way to improve VO2max/cardiorespiratory fitness a bit without assuming sedentary-person returns.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Your 180-day Whoop pattern shows avg strain 12.1 with 26.0% of days at >=14 strain, so I model one structured interval session as a modest CRF upgrade rather than a big new training load.

supplements.name: HIIT 3x/week
supplements.notes: Three HIIT sessions per week could be fine for some people, but for you I model it as roughly flat to slightly worse than 2x/week unless the extra stimulus clearly outperforms the recovery cost.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Three HIIT sessions per week could be fine for some people, but for you I model it as roughly flat to slightly worse than 2x/week unless the extra stimulus clearly outperforms the recovery cost.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: I allow a third interval session only as a near-flat extension of 2x/week, because your training load is already high and a 2025 frequency study found no clear extra benefit from 3x over 2x in recreational runners.

supplements.name: Ashwagandha 600mg
supplements.notes: Ashwagandha is best modeled as a stress/sleep/QOL intervention with non-zero rare downside.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Ashwagandha is best modeled as a stress/sleep/QOL intervention with non-zero rare downside.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: This is one of the few candidates I’d keep meaningfully positive because your sleep/stress profile leaves room for symptomatic benefit.

supplements.name: Custom oral appliance
supplements.notes: Custom oral appliance should usually underperform PAP on efficacy but can still be a credible option in mild OSA, especially if you prefer non-PAP treatment.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Custom oral appliance should usually underperform PAP on efficacy but can still be a credible option in mild OSA, especially if you prefer non-PAP treatment.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Your home study is already in the nonsevere range (REI 7.7/hr), so a custom oral appliance is now a concrete non-PAP option rather than a speculative backup.

supplements.name: Zone 2 cardio 2x/week
supplements.notes: Structured zone-2 work is still plausible as a small positive, but less likely than HIIT to create a meaningful new stimulus in your current routine.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Structured zone-2 work is still plausible as a small positive, but less likely than HIIT to create a meaningful new stimulus in your current routine.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Because you already run and train daily, I only give a small positive for making some cardio more intentionally aerobic and structured.

supplements.name: NAC 1200mg
supplements.notes: NAC remains speculative for broad prevention. Its respiratory upside is much more credible in chronic bronchitis or mucus-heavy phenotypes than in nasal-obstruction sleep problems.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: NAC remains speculative for broad prevention. Its respiratory upside is much more credible in chronic bronchitis or mucus-heavy phenotypes than in nasal-obstruction sleep problems.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Trimmed because your sleep pattern and recent response point more to an upper-airway/nasal issue than a mucus-heavy phenotype. NAC keeps some value for fatigue or secretions, but much less than airway-targeted measures.

supplements.name: NR 300mg
supplements.notes: NR is still mostly a mechanistic bet, with a small possible symptom pathway for fatigue / recovery.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: NR is still mostly a mechanistic bet, with a small possible symptom pathway for fatigue / recovery.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: I kept a small positive here only because there is at least one recent long-COVID RCT signal, but in healthier adults the literature is mostly NAD+ biomarker movement without obvious clinical payoff.

supplements.name: Strength maintenance
supplements.notes: Strength is important in general, but your marginal gain from formalizing it further appears small.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Strength is important in general, but your marginal gain from formalizing it further appears small.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Near flat because your described routine already contains daily strength work, so a separate strength-maintenance intervention adds little.

supplements.name: Ubiquinol 50mg
supplements.notes: CoQ10 can be useful in cardiac disease or statin myalgia, but that is not the main story here.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: CoQ10 can be useful in cardiac disease or statin myalgia, but that is not the main story here.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Near zero because the strongest CoQ10 evidence is in heart failure, which is not your phenotype.

supplements.name: Prebiotics combo
supplements.notes: Prebiotics may help GI comfort or satiety, but human hard-endpoint evidence is weak.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Prebiotics may help GI comfort or satiety, but human hard-endpoint evidence is weak.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Modeled as a gut-symptom / bowel-habit item rather than a mortality lever.

supplements.name: Lutein+Zeaxanthin
supplements.notes: Reasonable eye-health hedge, but the extrapolation to you is thin.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Reasonable eye-health hedge, but the extrapolation to you is thin.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Downweighted because AREDS2 is secondary prevention in older adults with existing AMD risk, not primary prevention for a 39-year-old.

supplements.name: Ginger 400mg
supplements.notes: Ginger looks mildly helpful, but not transformative. A much smaller downstream fall/fracture pathway is included with age gating.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Ginger looks mildly helpful, but not transformative. A much smaller downstream fall/fracture pathway is included with age gating.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Small positive only because human data support biomarker improvements and some joint-pain benefit, but your baseline does not show an obvious inflammatory pain phenotype. Any fall/fracture benefit is modeled as much smaller and grows mainly with age.

supplements.name: Boron 3mg
supplements.notes: Boron may matter for micronutrient biology, but not enough to give it a real QALY number beyond noise.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Boron may matter for micronutrient biology, but not enough to give it a real QALY number beyond noise.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Near zero because I could not justify a clinically meaningful human outcome effect here.

supplements.name: Nasal strips nightly
supplements.notes: Nasal strips can help if upper-airway narrowing is part of the problem, but they are usually an adjunct rather than a decisive treatment.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Nasal strips can help if upper-airway narrowing is part of the problem, but they are usually an adjunct rather than a decisive treatment.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Upweighted because your own notes say the first night with strips plus Nasacort gave zero snoring, but kept smaller than Nasacort because the evidence is mostly subjective-sleep benefit.

supplements.name: Broccoli Seed Ext 200mg
supplements.notes: Promising biology; still thin as a personalized QALY lever.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Promising biology; still thin as a personalized QALY lever.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Near zero because sulforaphane has mechanistic appeal but little direct hard-endpoint evidence in a healthy adult.

supplements.name: Lithium 1mg orotate
supplements.notes: Interesting hypothesis space, but not enough human intervention data for a large claim.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Interesting hypothesis space, but not enough human intervention data for a large claim.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Kept near zero because microdose lithium/orotate evidence is too thin to support a stronger estimate.

supplements.name: Lycopene 15mg
supplements.notes: Lycopene may be fine, but I do not see a strong supplement-specific QALY signal.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Lycopene may be fine, but I do not see a strong supplement-specific QALY signal.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Near zero because the cardiovascular literature is mainly observational and food-pattern confounding is hard to strip away.

supplements.name: Taurine 500mg top-up
supplements.notes: The marginal increment from 1.5g to 2g should not look large.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: The marginal increment from 1.5g to 2g should not look large.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Very small because this is only a top-up on top of Longevity Mix, not a full taurine intervention.

supplements.name: Astaxanthin 12mg
supplements.notes: Interesting antioxidant biomarker story, but weak evidence for durable clinical payoff in someone like you.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Interesting antioxidant biomarker story, but weak evidence for durable clinical payoff in someone like you.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Kept near zero because the human literature is mostly biomarker and specialty-population work.

supplements.name: Spermidine 10mg
supplements.notes: Spermidine is still more of a longevity hypothesis than a demonstrated human benefit.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Spermidine is still more of a longevity hypothesis than a demonstrated human benefit.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Near zero because the better human RCT found no memory benefit despite strong preclinical enthusiasm.

supplements.name: Luteolin 100mg
supplements.notes: Luteolin remains mostly a mechanistic / preclinical longevity ingredient.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Luteolin remains mostly a mechanistic / preclinical longevity ingredient.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Near zero because I could not justify a meaningful clinical effect from current human outcome data.

supplements.name: Apigenin 50mg
supplements.notes: Apigenin is a reasonable sleep-stack experiment, but not a proven life-extension tool.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Apigenin is a reasonable sleep-stack experiment, but not a proven life-extension tool.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Kept positive only through plausible calming/sleep utility, not through a strong causal longevity claim.

supplements.name: Fisetin 100mg
supplements.notes: Strong marketing and interesting biology, but not enough human evidence for a positive ground-up estimate.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Strong marketing and interesting biology, but not enough human evidence for a positive ground-up estimate.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Modeled as flat because fisetin is still largely a senolytic hypothesis in humans.

supplements.name: Doxepin 3mg
supplements.notes: Low-dose doxepin has better insomnia-guideline support than trazodone for sleep maintenance, but it remains a symptom treatment rather than an airway fix.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Low-dose doxepin has better insomnia-guideline support than trazodone for sleep maintenance, but it remains a symptom treatment rather than an airway fix.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Modeled as a better-targeted sleep-maintenance bridge than trazodone, but still with some hangover and respiratory caution rather than assuming it is free upside.

supplements.name: Vitamin K2 MK-7+MK-4
supplements.notes: Vitamin K2 is a weak preventive bet at your age unless there is a clearer bone-risk story.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Vitamin K2 is a weak preventive bet at your age unless there is a clearer bone-risk story.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Kept near zero because the more favorable fracture/BMD data are mostly in older postmenopausal populations.

supplements.name: Vitamin C 500mg (extra)
supplements.notes: No reason to expect much marginal benefit here.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: No reason to expect much marginal benefit here.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Incremental vitamin C on top of adequate intake should be near flat.

supplements.name: Humidifier nightly
supplements.notes: A bedroom humidifier is modeled as a modest nasal-comfort adjunct, not a real OSA treatment. It is most attractive when the room is actually dry or you wake with dry irritated nasal passages.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: A bedroom humidifier is modeled as a modest nasal-comfort adjunct, not a real OSA treatment. It is most attractive when the room is actually dry or you wake with dry irritated nasal passages.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Kept small because your current evidence points more to upper-airway obstruction and nasal inflammation than to dry-air irritation alone; nasal-inflammation probability is 0.47.

cost_assumptions.notes: Personal night-only estimate for a Honeywell HEV620B already owned by Max. Selected annual cost includes electricity plus steady-state hardware amortization, but not replacement wick filters.

supplements.name: Curcumin 250mg
supplements.notes: Curcumin is better supported as an anti-inflammatory biomarker intervention than a proven longevity intervention.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Curcumin is better supported as an anti-inflammatory biomarker intervention than a proven longevity intervention.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Kept small because dose is modest and the strongest human data are for biomarker shifts or disease-specific pain populations.

supplements.name: TMG/Betaine 1g
supplements.notes: Hard to justify more than noise-level utility.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Hard to justify more than noise-level utility.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Tiny because this is mostly a methylation-support adjunct, not a clinically demonstrated intervention.

supplements.name: Creatine 5g
supplements.notes: Creatine has decent functional evidence, but most of the compelling data are performance / body-composition and older-adult contexts rather than mortality.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Creatine has decent functional evidence, but most of the compelling data are performance / body-composition and older-adult contexts rather than mortality.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Kept positive for muscle/performance resilience, but trimmed because you are 39 rather than sarcopenic and because creatinine/eGFR make me avoid giving it a free pass.

supplements.name: Quercetin 500mg
supplements.notes: Quercetin might matter in the right symptom cluster, but not as a broad longevity capsule.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Quercetin might matter in the right symptom cluster, but not as a broad longevity capsule.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Kept small because the best case is symptom relief in inflammatory/viral-persistence settings, not generic prevention.

supplements.name: Low-dose lithium 5mg
supplements.notes: Interesting neuropsychiatric hedge, but still thin as a quantified longevity intervention.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Interesting neuropsychiatric hedge, but still thin as a quantified longevity intervention.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Kept small because the low-dose human outcome case is still mostly ecological and indirect.

supplements.name: Garlic 1200mg
supplements.notes: Garlic is a plausible small cardiometabolic adjunct, not a major longevity mover for you.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Garlic is a plausible small cardiometabolic adjunct, not a major longevity mover for you.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Downweighted because garlic's BP signal is clearest in hypertensive adults and you are not documented hypertensive.

supplements.name: Black seed oil 1g
supplements.notes: Interesting anti-inflammatory profile, weak quantified personal-health case.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Interesting anti-inflammatory profile, weak quantified personal-health case.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Kept near zero because evidence remains mostly biomarker and specialty-population work.

supplements.name: Zinc carnosine 75mg
supplements.notes: Zinc carnosine is a phenotype-specific gut-symptom intervention, not a broad longevity tool.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Zinc carnosine is a phenotype-specific gut-symptom intervention, not a broad longevity tool.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Useful mainly if you have a real GI barrier/irritation problem; otherwise near zero.

supplements.name: Trazodone 50mg
supplements.notes: Trazodone looks like a symptomatic sleep/QOL tool here, not a credible mortality intervention.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Trazodone looks like a symptomatic sleep/QOL tool here, not a credible mortality intervention.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Upweighted because sleep is still clearly below target, but netted against hangover / dependency / long-run medication burden rather than assuming all sleep-med utility is durable.

supplements.name: Alpha-lipoic acid 300mg
supplements.notes: Weak general-prevention case.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Weak general-prevention case.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Near zero because the best-supported use case is diabetic neuropathy, not your phenotype.

supplements.name: EGCG 400mg (green tea)
supplements.notes: Green-tea epidemiology should not become a large EGCG-capsule claim.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Green-tea epidemiology should not become a large EGCG-capsule claim.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Near zero or slightly negative because liver-risk transport is clearer than mortality benefit transport in a supplement user like you.

supplements.name: Ubiquinol 50mg (unbundled)
supplements.notes: Standalone ubiquinol should inherit the same weak phenotype-specific estimate as bundled ubiquinol.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Standalone ubiquinol should inherit the same weak phenotype-specific estimate as bundled ubiquinol.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Same small CoQ10 estimate as the bundled version; the standalone question is mostly about whether it is worth buying separately.

supplements.name: Luteolin 100mg (unbundled)
supplements.notes: Standalone luteolin should inherit the same mechanistic-only estimate as bundled luteolin.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Standalone luteolin should inherit the same mechanistic-only estimate as bundled luteolin.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Same near-zero estimate as bundled luteolin; useful mainly to compare whether buying it separately makes any sense.

supplements.name: Cistanche 200mg
supplements.notes: Cistanche is a plausible functional-performance bet with weak hard-outcome evidence.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Cistanche is a plausible functional-performance bet with weak hard-outcome evidence.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Small positive through possible exercise/recovery utility, not because I trust a direct longevity story.

supplements.name: Pterostilbene 50mg
supplements.notes: Little reason to assign a meaningful QALY effect.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Little reason to assign a meaningful QALY effect.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Near zero because this is still a resveratrol-family hypothesis, not a robust human outcome intervention.

supplements.name: Fisetin 100mg (unbundled)
supplements.notes: There is still no strong human basis for a meaningful fisetin QALY claim.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: There is still no strong human basis for a meaningful fisetin QALY claim.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Same skeptical estimate as bundled fisetin; standalone purchase is mostly a cost question.

supplements.name: Sulforaphane 20mg (extra)
supplements.notes: The extra dose is mostly mechanistic optimism.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: The extra dose is mostly mechanistic optimism.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Incremental-only because you already get some sulforaphane exposure from food and the bundled broccoli seed extract.

supplements.name: PQQ 20mg
supplements.notes: Should be near zero unless future evidence improves materially.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Should be near zero unless future evidence improves materially.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Mostly a mitochondrial-biomarker bet with little direct human utility evidence.

supplements.name: Acarbose 50mg
supplements.notes: Acarbose is more likely to create hassle than durable value for your current phenotype.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Acarbose is more likely to create hassle than durable value for your current phenotype.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Modeled as mildly negative because GI burden transports better than lifespan-mouse optimism.

supplements.name: Mouth tape nightly
supplements.notes: Mouth tape is modeled as a plausible adjunct if habitual open-mouth breathing is part of the problem, not as a broad OSA treatment. The direct evidence is small and mostly in mild OSA or snoring.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Mouth tape is modeled as a plausible adjunct if habitual open-mouth breathing is part of the problem, not as a broad OSA treatment. The direct evidence is small and mostly in mild OSA or snoring.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Upweighted because you now have confirmed mild OSA plus a strong recent airway-response pattern, but kept below strips and head elevation because mouth tape only really makes sense if mouth breathing is part of the phenotype and your data still point heavily to nasal and upper-airway contributors; upper-airway probability is 0.695.

supplements.name: Lions Mane 1g
supplements.notes: Lion’s Mane is plausible as a small cognitive/QOL bet, not a large life-extension lever.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Lion’s Mane is plausible as a small cognitive/QOL bet, not a large life-extension lever.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Small only: human evidence is mainly cognition/mood signal, not durable prevention.

supplements.name: High-EPA Omega-3 +2g
supplements.notes: Some cardiometabolic plausibility remains, but the incremental value over your existing health profile is modest.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Some cardiometabolic plausibility remains, but the incremental value over your existing health profile is modest.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Marginally more plausible than low-dose omega-3 because triglycerides are not perfect, but still sharply trimmed at your baseline risk.

supplements.name: Ergothioneine 5mg
supplements.notes: Interesting biomarker story, weak supplement-level clinical story.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Interesting biomarker story, weak supplement-level clinical story.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Near zero because most of the case is still observational and nutrient-status based.

supplements.name: Statin (rosuvastatin 5mg)
supplements.notes: Statins are one of the more credible preventive drug classes, but the marginal benefit for a lean 39-year-old with already-good lipids is much smaller than headline meta-analytic averages.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Statins are one of the more credible preventive drug classes, but the marginal benefit for a lean 39-year-old with already-good lipids is much smaller than headline meta-analytic averages.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Strong causal class, but you already have LDL 64 and no documented ASCVD, so most trial effects shrink hard on transport.

supplements.name: Omega-3 CLO ~500mg
supplements.notes: The marginal benefit of low-dose cod liver oil looks small at your baseline risk.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: The marginal benefit of low-dose cod liver oil looks small at your baseline risk.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Strongly downweighted because this is a low dose and your LDL/HbA1c are already favorable.
supplements.db_product: Nordic Naturals Arctic Cod Liver Oil

supplements.name: GHK-Cu peptide (topical)
supplements.notes: Topical GHK-Cu may help skin appearance, but not enough to justify a large healthspan estimate.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Topical GHK-Cu may help skin appearance, but not enough to justify a large healthspan estimate.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: This is mostly aesthetic/skin utility, which is real but should stay modest in a QALY-only model.

supplements.name: Metformin 500mg
supplements.notes: Metformin is plausible as a modest metabolic-risk intervention, but not a big generic longevity lever for your phenotype.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Metformin is plausible as a modest metabolic-risk intervention, but not a big generic longevity lever for your phenotype.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Downweighted heavily because your glycemia is already good and most compelling outcome data are in diabetic or prediabetic populations.

supplements.name: Low-dose aspirin 81mg
supplements.notes: Low-dose aspirin is now mostly a narrow-risk tool, not a generic prevention default.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Low-dose aspirin is now mostly a narrow-risk tool, not a generic prevention default.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: The bleeding downside transports better to you than the net-prevention upside, because you are young and low-risk rather than high-ASCVD.

supplements.name: NMN 500mg
supplements.notes: Mechanistic appeal is stronger than demonstrated clinical value.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Mechanistic appeal is stronger than demonstrated clinical value.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Near zero because NR/NMN remain mostly NAD-biomarker interventions in humans.

supplements.name: NR 300mg (unbundled)
supplements.notes: Standalone NR should inherit the same tiny clinical estimate as bundled NR, with cost deciding the verdict.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Standalone NR should inherit the same tiny clinical estimate as bundled NR, with cost deciding the verdict.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Same biology as bundled NR; the question here is whether it is worth buying as a standalone product.

supplements.name: 17α-estradiol (topical)
supplements.notes: No strong basis for a meaningful human QALY claim here yet.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: No strong basis for a meaningful human QALY claim here yet.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Near zero because this is still mostly mouse lifespan extrapolation.

supplements.name: Vitamin D 2000 IU
supplements.notes: Vitamin D looks more like a deficiency correction tool than an additional-optimization tool at your current level.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Vitamin D looks more like a deficiency correction tool than an additional-optimization tool at your current level.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Almost fully downweighted because your latest 25(OH)D is 51.5 ng/mL, already in a replete range.

supplements.name: Berberine 500mg
supplements.notes: Berberine is not an attractive personal intervention at your baseline.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Berberine is not an attractive personal intervention at your baseline.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Your glycemia is already good, so the GI downside matters more than the diabetes-trial upside.

supplements.name: Cocoa flavanols ~500mg
supplements.notes: Some plausible cardiometabolic value, but your current risk profile leaves less headroom.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Some plausible cardiometabolic value, but your current risk profile leaves less headroom.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Downweighted because LDL and glycemia are already good and COSMOS enrolled much older adults.

supplements.name: Finasteride 1.25mg
supplements.notes: Hair-loss treatment has meaningful psychosocial value for some men, but very little credible mortality effect.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Hair-loss treatment has meaningful psychosocial value for some men, but very little credible mortality effect.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Modeled almost entirely as QOL: hair preservation seems clearly valued, but I netted that against sexual-side-effect risk rather than assuming pure upside.

supplements.name: Hyaluronic acid (oral)
supplements.notes: Oral hyaluronic acid may have symptom value, but it looks phenotype-specific. A much smaller downstream fall/fracture pathway is included with age gating.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Oral hyaluronic acid may have symptom value, but it looks phenotype-specific. A much smaller downstream fall/fracture pathway is included with age gating.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Downweighted because oral HA benefits are mostly in chronic pain / joint-discomfort populations. Any fall/fracture benefit is modeled as much smaller and grows mainly with age.

supplements.name: Collagen 22g
supplements.notes: Collagen may help joint or skin outcomes in the right phenotype, but your baseline does not scream high-yield collagen responder. A much smaller downstream fall/fracture pathway is included with age gating.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Collagen may help joint or skin outcomes in the right phenotype, but your baseline does not scream high-yield collagen responder. A much smaller downstream fall/fracture pathway is included with age gating.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Strongly downweighted because the better human data are in osteoarthritis / meniscopathy, not healthy adults without documented joint disease. Any fall/fracture benefit is modeled as much smaller and grows mainly with age.

supplements.name: Rapamycin 5mg/wk
supplements.notes: Rapamycin remains interesting, but human longevity evidence is too incomplete for a large positive estimate.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Rapamycin remains interesting, but human longevity evidence is too incomplete for a large positive estimate.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: I am treating this mainly as an uncertain translational hypothesis, not a currently supported personal-health intervention.

supplements.name: Urolithin A 500mg
supplements.notes: Urolithin A is plausible but still early and expensive.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Urolithin A is plausible but still early and expensive.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: I kept a small functional upside for exercise recovery / mitochondria, but not enough to justify a major QALY number.

supplements.name: BPC-157 cycle
supplements.notes: For a healthy user, BPC-157 should be modeled as near-zero or slightly negative until real human efficacy and product-quality evidence improve.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: For a healthy user, BPC-157 should be modeled as near-zero or slightly negative until real human efficacy and product-quality evidence improve.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Without a concrete injury or ulcer-healing phenotype, BPC-157 is mostly gray-market uncertainty and injection burden.

supplements.name: TB-500 cycle
supplements.notes: TB-500 looks like a gray-market hypothesis stack component, not a credible general-health intervention.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: TB-500 looks like a gray-market hypothesis stack component, not a credible general-health intervention.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: This is even more speculative than BPC-157 in the absence of a specific recovery use case.

supplements.name: HBOT 60-session course
supplements.notes: Interesting but weakly grounded for a healthy 39-year-old; the evidence does not justify a large QALY estimate.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Interesting but weakly grounded for a healthy 39-year-old; the evidence does not justify a large QALY estimate.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: I am treating HBOT as a high-cost surrogate-biomarker play with uncertain persistence, not as a demonstrated healthy-aging intervention.

supplements.name: Traditional dry sauna 4x/week
supplements.notes: Traditional dry sauna is the most plausible of the classic biohacker add-ons, but the credible benefit for you still looks modest.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Traditional dry sauna is the most plausible of the classic biohacker add-ons, but the credible benefit for you still looks modest.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: You already exercise daily and your cardiometabolic baseline is good, so I treat sauna as a modest relaxation/recovery and BP-surrogate intervention rather than as a real direct-longevity claim.

supplements.name: Infrared sauna 4x/week
supplements.notes: Infrared sauna may help relaxation or recovery a bit, but the evidence is materially weaker than for Finnish-style dry sauna.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Infrared sauna may help relaxation or recovery a bit, but the evidence is materially weaker than for Finnish-style dry sauna.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: I treat infrared sauna as a weaker recovery/relaxation analog to traditional dry sauna rather than as an equivalent longevity intervention.

supplements.name: SGLT2i (empagliflozin)
supplements.notes: SGLT2 inhibitors are clinically important in the right phenotype, but they should not look like a major longevity drug for you.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: SGLT2 inhibitors are clinically important in the right phenotype, but they should not look like a major longevity drug for you.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Transport is extremely weak here because the flagship benefits are in diabetes, heart failure, and CKD populations rather than a healthy lean 39-year-old.

supplements.name: Lemborexant 5mg
supplements.notes: Lemborexant looks like a credible evidence-based trazodone alternative in mild OSA, with stronger sleep-maintenance efficacy than doxepin and less respiratory discomfort than suvorexant.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Lemborexant looks like a credible evidence-based trazodone alternative in mild OSA, with stronger sleep-maintenance efficacy than doxepin and less respiratory discomfort than suvorexant.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Modeled as a strong maintenance-insomnia candidate with actual OSA respiratory-safety data, but probably a bit more next-day drag than daridorexant and likely not covered on your plan.

supplements.name: Suvorexant 10mg
supplements.notes: Suvorexant is still a plausible maintenance-insomnia option, but the respiratory-safety story in OSA is less clean, so it ranks below the other DORAs for you.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Suvorexant is still a plausible maintenance-insomnia option, but the respiratory-safety story in OSA is less clean, so it ranks below the other DORAs for you.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Modeled as meaningfully better than trazodone on mechanism, but with more OSA-specific respiratory caution and next-day somnolence risk than daridorexant or lemborexant.

supplements.name: Daridorexant 25mg
supplements.notes: Daridorexant looks like a more evidence-aligned insomnia alternative than trazodone in mild OSA, especially if you want less generic sedation.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: Daridorexant looks like a more evidence-aligned insomnia alternative than trazodone in mild OSA, especially if you want less generic sedation.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Modeled as the cleanest trazodone replacement candidate because it targets maintenance insomnia and has direct mild-to-moderate OSA respiratory-safety evidence, but its cost is brutal.

supplements.name: GLP-1 RA (semaglutide)
supplements.notes: For you this is closer to a weakly justified, harm-prone off-label bet than a real healthspan intervention.
supplements.ground_up_rationale: For you this is closer to a weakly justified, harm-prone off-label bet than a real healthspan intervention.
supplements.ground_up_personalization: Your BMI and likely body-fat profile do not resemble the obesity/CVD populations where semaglutide shows the clearest benefit.
